name: Yosi Piamenta
description:
'''Yosi Piamenta''' ({{langx|he|יוסי פיאמנטה}}; 29 November 1951 – 23 August 2015) was an Orthodox Jewish singer-songwriter and guitarist, known for introducing the electric guitar to [[Jewish music]]. Piamenta played [[rock and roll]] tunes, often infused with heavy rock [[lick (music)|licks]] and extended [[guitar solos]] – all while dressed in Orthodox Jewish [[religious clothing]] and singing [[Biblical Hebrew]] lyrics. Piamenta was widely acknowledged by rock critics as a guitar virtuoso.

In addition to being an original songwriter, Piamenta covered others' music in the [[religious Jewish music]] category – his album ''Songs of the Rebbes'' includes various [[Chabad|Lubavitch]], [[Belz (Hasidic dynasty)|Belz]], and [[Sephardi]] [[nigun]]im and [[zemirot]] – as well as secular American music, such as [[Eric Clapton]], [[Carlos Santana]], and [[Jimi Hendrix]]. Piamenta described his music style saying, "I do [[klezmer]] with electric guitar".
